legal_definition.text: Acid attack victim means a person disfigured due to violent assaults by throwing of acid or similar corrosive substance.
legal_definition.statutory_source: Schedule to the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

statutory_status.act: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

benchmark_requirement.definition: Person with benchmark disability means a person with not less than forty per cent of a specified disability as certified.
benchmark_requirement.assessment_note: Assessment based on extent of permanent disfigurement and functional impairment as per notified medical guidelines.

reservation_category.grouping_logic: Included within the cluster of specified physical disabilities for reservation in Government establishments.
reservation_category.eligibility_condition: Reservation applicable only for benchmark disability.

certification_workflow.assessment_guidelines: Evaluation under medical guidelines notified by Central Government (Section 56).

distinction_framework: Applies specifically to victims of corrosive substance attacks.
Disability arises from permanent disfigurement and/or functional impairment.
Reservation grouping does not merge statutory identity.
legal_boundary_conditions: Reservation applicable only for benchmark disability.
Certification must confirm permanent impact of acid attack.
Certification valid throughout India unless lawfully revoked.
interpretive_notes: Recognition focuses on permanent physical disfigurement and functional consequences.
May overlap with visual or locomotor impairment depending on injury extent.
Legal protections extend beyond reservation to education, employment, dignity and accessibility.

legal_schema.law_name: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act
